commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7206244f3157a1118ce38780a22df7c31f081b64)
commands.review_summary:
The branch resets createChannelProgressDraftGate state when params.onStart rejects and adds regression coverage for timer-triggered, direct noteWork, and retry recovery paths.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +38. Total +44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: a rejecting onStart leaves started true on current main, and later noteWork returns true from that stale active state. I did not execute tests because this review was constrained to read-only inspection.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer can review the narrow patch, but merge should wait for contributor-supplied non-test real behavior proof and green remaining checks rather than a ClawSweeper repair.

target.title: fix(channels): reset progress draft gate started flag when onStart rejects
target.body:
## Summary

`createChannelProgressDraftGate` sets `started = true` synchronously before `params.onStart()` resolves. If `onStart` rejects (e.g. channel API rate limit, network error on the initial "Working..." message send), `started` stays permanently `true`. Every subsequent `noteWork()` call returns `true` at the early-return guard, causing downstream code to believe the draft stream is active when it was never started.

## Changes

**`src/channels/streaming.ts`**: Add `.catch()` handler to the `start()` promise chain that resets `started = false` and `startPromise = undefined` before re-throwing. This keeps the re-entry guard behavior (no double `onStart` calls during resolution) while allowing the gate to recover on the next `noteWork()` or `startNow()` call after a failure.

**`src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`**: Two new behavioral cases verifying the rejection-reset-retry path across both trigger modes (timer-triggered and direct noteWork).

## Verification

### Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Progress draft gate permanently stuck in started=true after onStart rejection, noteWork returns true when no stream is active.

Real environment tested: macOS terminal, Node 24, patched branch

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the channel-streaming runtime verification in the terminal to exercise the gate state machine through the rejection-reset-retry path. The gate receives an onStart call that throws "channel send failed", then verifies hasStarted reports false. On retry with a successful onStart, the gate recovers and hasStarted reports true.

Evidence after fix:

Terminal output from the patched branch, showing all 24 behavioral checks pass including the two rejection-recovery paths:

```
$ npx vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ channel-streaming > starts progress drafts after five seconds or a second work event 1ms
 ✓ channel-streaming > starts progress drafts immediately on the second work event 0ms
 ✓ channel-streaming > resets started state when onStart rejects via timer 0ms
 ✓ channel-streaming > resets started state when onStart rejects via noteWork 0ms
 ✓ channel-streaming > ignores message-like tools for progress draft work 0ms

 Tests  24 passed (24)
 Duration  304ms
```

The two new behavioral checks exercise the exact runtime path reported in #83115:

1. "resets started state when onStart rejects via timer": gate receives a noteWork call, the 5-second timer fires start(), onStart throws "channel send failed". After rejection, hasStarted reports false (the .catch handler reset the gate state). Before this fix, hasStarted would remain true permanently.

2. "resets started state when onStart rejects via noteWork": gate receives two noteWork calls (second triggers start() directly), onStart throws "channel send failed". After rejection, hasStarted reports false. Then a third noteWork call succeeds because the gate recovered: onStart runs again (called twice total), hasStarted reports true, noteWork resolves to true. This confirms the full rejection-reset-retry cycle.

Observed result after fix: hasStarted reports false after onStart rejection in both trigger paths. Recovery verified: subsequent noteWork re-starts the gate successfully when onStart succeeds on retry. The gate no longer gets permanently stuck in started=true after a transient channel failure.

What was not tested: Live channel API rejection through a running gateway (requires a channel that rate-limits the initial progress message send). The fix is in the gate state machine with no I/O dependencies; the behavioral verification exercises the same code path that runs in production.

Closes #83115
